verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein of the CRISP family, expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen. The incomplete summary indicates it binds to sperm and participates in sperm-egg recognition and fusion. While primarily a secreted protein, CRISPs are known to associate with sperm plasma membranes (acquired from epididymal secretions during sperm maturation) and to interact with egg surface receptors during fertilization. This represents stable, functionally relevant surface association in the context of fertilization and sperm maturation, qualifying as contextual surface accessibility via a tissue-restricted, process-specific mechanism rather than constitutive surface residence.
